Well, why aren't I making $10 million a year, which I'm not in case you're curious? Well, because I make some decisions about it. It's not because I couldn't. I make some decisions about it, because that's a different list of requirements. And I look at that list and I say, I don't know, the list of rewards for me, between where I'm at and what I can do now and where I'd be and what I could do there, is not enough to motivate me, at least at this moment, to want to meet that list of requirements. And so that's perfectly okay. Most people never go through this exercise. They never really think through, well, okay, if I'm going to own this and I'm going to make this and I'm going to do this, what are the requirements in order to be there? And of course, the vast majority of people are really taught there are no requirements. It's luck of the draw. It's who's in the right place at the right time. Or that it's all talent or it's all skill and it's none of those. So it's perfectly okay to make a decision that you don't want to meet these requirements. I think one of the biggest ones that people have the most trouble with is this one about how others think about you. I'm in the neighborhood of 155. And so here, so who do you think probably a matrix of the population and all that? Who do you think is like the most popular, most universally known celebrity in America? Oprah. And as a celebrity, who makes the most money? Oprah. We know that from the ranking list, right? Also, who gets crept on the most? All. I mean, on and on and on and on and on. The entire Charlie Givens business, if you know any history of that, was all destroyed by one nitwit couple who claimed, incorrectly, incidentally, that the Charlie Givens personal finance training taught them to cancel all their insurance. And then they had a car wreck and one of them was like crippled and they had no insurance. And of course, the training actually is look for duplicate coverages. For example, you may have health insurance and you may have a health insurance rider on your car policy and you really don't need both of them. But that caused a 2020 show all about the cripple on the board with the four wheels being pushed out by the distraught husband.
